Implement the following plan: # Phase System for Apartment Blueprint Editor ## Context The editor currently has no concept of construction/design phases. All building elements render unconditionally. This plan adds a phase system so objects can be assigned to phases (e.g., "Demolition", "New Construction", "Finishes") and filtered by three viewing modes. Phases are project-level, user-assignable, and view-only filters — they never alter actual model data. --- ## 1. Domain Model — New Files ### `src/domain/phaseModels.js` (NEW) ```js createPhase(name, order, color) → { id, name, order, color, visible: true } createDefaultPhase() → createPhase('Base Design', 0, '#4A90D9') sortPhases(phases) → sorted by order ascending getOrderedPhases(project) → sortPhases(project.phases || []) getNextPhaseOrder(phases) → max(order) + 1 reorderPhases(phases, movedId, newOrder) → re-indexes all order values PHASE_COLORS = ['#4A90D9', '#E07B39', '#5BAE5B', '#C75D8E', '#8B6EC1', '#D4A843', '#44B4B4', '#D05555'] ``` Uses `generateId('phase')` from `src/domain/ids.js`. ### `src/domain/phaseFilter.js` (NEW) Pure functions — no React dependencies. Single source of truth for filtering. ```js PHASE_VIEW = { ALL: 'all', SINGLE: 'single', CUMULATIVE: 'cumulative' } filterFloorByPhase(floor, phases, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → returns shallow-copy floor with filtered arrays → ALL mode: returns floor unchanged → SINGLE mode: keeps objects where phaseId === activePhaseId or phaseId is null → CUMULATIVE mode: keeps objects where phase.order <= activePhase.order or phaseId is null → Also filters doors/windows whose parent wall was filtered out isObjectVisibleInPhase(obj, phases, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → returns boolean for a single object filterProjectByPhase(project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → returns project with all floors filtered (for 3D/elevation/section) ``` Objects with `phaseId === null` (unassigned) are **always visible** in all modes. ### `src/hooks/usePhaseFilteredFloor.js` (NEW) ```js usePhaseFilteredFloor(floor, project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → useMemo wrapping filterFloorByPhase ``` --- ## 2. Model Changes — Existing Files ### `src/domain/models.js` - `createProject`: add `phases: [createDefaultPhase()]` to return object - Import `createDefaultPhase` from `phaseModels.js` - **No changes to individual object factories** — `phaseId` is set at dispatch time ### `src/persistence/deserialize.js` - After existing backfill logic, add: - If `project.phases` is missing/empty, set to `[createDefaultPhase()]` - Validate each phase has `id, name, order, color, visible` - No object-level migration needed — missing `phaseId` treated as `null` (always visible) --- ## 3. State Management ### `src/app/ProjectProvider.jsx` New reducer actions: | Action | Behavior | |--------|----------| | `PHASE_ADD` | Push new phase to `project.phases`, re-sort | | `PHASE_UPDATE` | Merge updates by phase id | | `PHASE_DELETE` | Remove phase; set `phaseId = null` on all objects across all floors that reference it | | `PHASE_REORDER` | Update order values, re-sort | The `PHASE_DELETE` cascade iterates all floors and all object arrays (walls, doors, windows, columns, beams, slabs, stairs, landings, fixtures, rooms, railings) to nullify orphaned `phaseId` references. Pattern follows `FLOOR_DELETE` cascade. ### `src/app/EditorProvider.jsx` New state fields in `createInitialEditorState`: ```js activePhaseId: null, // selected phase for viewing/assigning phaseViewMode: 'all', // 'all' | 'single' | 'cumulative' ``` New reducer actions: - `SET_ACTIVE_PHASE` → sets `activePhaseId` - `SET_PHASE_VIEW_MODE` → sets `phaseViewMode` Add `useEffect` to sync `activePhaseId` when project phases change (similar to `activeFloorId` sync with `availableFloorIds`). Pass `availablePhaseIds` as a prop. --- ## 4. Object Phase Assignment ### `src/editor/useEditorTool.js` - Add `activePhaseId` to the `ctx` object passed to all handlers (line 54-58) - Pull from editor state (already available via `useEditor()` in the call site — `SvgCanvas.jsx`) ### All editor handlers (11 files) Each handler creates objects and dispatches ADD actions. After creating the object, set `phaseId`: ```js // Example in wallDrawHandler.js line 62-66: const wall = createWall(toolState.start, endPt, undefined, { ... }); wall.phaseId = ctx.activePhaseId || null; // ← add this line dispatch({ type: 'WALL_ADD', floorId: activeFloorId, wall }); ``` Files to modify: - `src/editor/handlers/wallDrawHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/doorPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/windowPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/columnPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/beamPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/slabPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/stairPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/landingPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/fixturePlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/roomPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/railingPlaceHandler.js` Also handle auto-detect rooms in Toolbar.jsx (the "Detect Rooms" button). --- ## 5. Rendering Integration ### Plan View — `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` - Import `usePhaseFilteredFloor` - After `const floor = getFloor(activeFloorId)` (line 71), compute: ```js const filteredFloor = usePhaseFilteredFloor(floor, project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode); ``` - Pass `filteredFloor` to all renderers (lines 348-364) instead of `floor` - Keep `floor` (unfiltered) for `SelectionOverlay` and tool previews so selection/interaction still works on all objects - Pass `activePhaseId` and `phaseViewMode` to `useEditorTool` via editor state ### Elevation View — `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` (line 409-416) - Pass filtered project to `ElevationRenderer`: ```jsx ``` - Where `filteredProject` is computed via `filterProjectByPhase(project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode)` ### Section View — `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` (line 403-407) - Same pattern: pass `filteredProject` and `filteredFloor` to `SectionRenderer` ### 3D Preview — `src/three/viewer/ThreePreviewPanel.jsx` - Import `filterProjectByPhase` from `phaseFilter.js` - Access `activePhaseId` and `phaseViewMode` from `useEditor()` - Apply filter before building scene descriptor: ```js const filteredProject = useMemo( () => filterProjectByPhase(project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode), [project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode] ); ``` - Pass `filteredProject` to `buildPreviewScene` (line 35-38) ### Sheet Sources — `src/sheets/sources.js` - `collectPlanPoints` and viewport source builders receive floor data - Apply `filterFloorByPhase` inside `buildPlanSource`, `buildSectionSource`, `buildElevationSource` - Pass phase state through viewport source resolution (from the sheet rendering context) - Sheets initially use the current editor phase view mode; future enhancement can add per-viewport overrides ### Export — `src/export/sheetExport.js` - **No changes needed** — export captures the already-filtered rendered SVG/canvas --- ## 6. UI Changes ### Sidebar — `src/ui/Sidebar.jsx` Add a **"Phases"** collapsible section after "Sheets" and before the floor-context sections. Follows the same section pattern as Floors/Sheets. **Contents:** - Phase view mode segmented control: `[All] [Single] [Cumulative]` - Phase list with each item showing: - Color dot (circle with phase color) - Phase name - Visibility eye toggle - Up/down reorder arrows - Delete button - Click to select as active phase (highlighted with accent border like floor cards) - "+" button to add new phase (auto-named "Phase N", next color from palette) ### Sidebar CSS — `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` - `.phaseItem` / `.phaseItemActive` — item card styling (follows `.floorCard` pattern) - `.phaseColorDot` — 12px circle with phase color - `.phaseViewControl` — segmented control (follows existing segment pattern) ### Properties Panel — `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx` **PhaseSelector component** (defined inside PropertiesPanel or extracted to separate file): ```jsx function PhaseSelector({ phaseId, phases, onChange }) { return (
Phase
); } ``` Add `PhaseSelector` to all 11 object property panels: Wall, Door, Window, Column, Beam, Slab, Stair, Landing, Fixture, Room, Railing. **PhaseProperties panel** — when a phase is selected via sidebar (`selectedType === 'phase'`): - Phase name (InputField) - Phase color (color picker — `` + hex input, matching room color pattern) - Phase order (read-only) - Object count summary ### Properties Panel CSS — `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.module.css` - Phase selector dropdown styling (matches existing `

# Modular Apartment Planner - Project Memory

## Tech Stack
- React 19 + Vite 6, JavaScript (no TypeScript), CSS Modules, SVG rendering, localStorage persistence
- Path alias: `@/` -> `src/`

## Architecture
- **Domain models**: Plain JS objects via factory functions in `src/domain/models.js`
- **State**: Two React Contexts with useReducer - `ProjectProvider` (model data) + `EditorProvider` (UI/interaction state)
- **Coordinates**: All model values in millimeters. SVG uses translate+scale transform. Default zoom: 0.1
- **Editor tools**: Handler pattern - each tool is an object with `onMouseDown/Move/Up/KeyDown/getCursor`
- **Rendering**: SVG-based, z-order: Grid -> Rooms -> Walls -> Doors/Windows -> Dimensions -> Selection -> Preview

## Key File Locations
- Entry: `src/main.jsx`, `src/app/App.jsx`
- Models: `src/domain/models.js`, `src/domain/defaults.js`
- State: `src/app/ProjectProvider.jsx`, `src/app/EditorProvider.jsx`
- Geometry: `src/geometry/` (point, line, polygon, wallGeometry)
- Renderers: `src/renderers/` (SvgCanvas, WallRenderer, DoorRenderer, etc.)
- Editor handlers: `src/editor/handlers/` (selectHandler, wallDrawHandler, doorPlaceHandler, windowPlaceHandler)
- UI: `src/ui/` (Toolbar, Sidebar, PropertiesPanel, InputField, Modal)
- Persistence: `src/persistence/` (serialize, deserialize, storage)

## ID Format
`prefix_timestamp36_counter36` (e.g., `wall_m1abc_1`)

## Keyboard Shortcuts
V=Select, W=Wall, D=Door, N=Window, Escape=cancel, Delete=remove, Ctrl+S=save

- ProjectProvider actions: PHASE_ADD, PHASE_UPDATE, PHASE_DELETE, PHASE_REORDER
- All 11 handlers set `phaseId` on created objects; Toolbar's Detect Rooms also sets it
- SvgCanvas uses filteredFloor for renderers, unfiltered floor for selection/previews
- 3D preview (ThreePreviewPanel) also filters via `filterProjectByPhase`

# Phase System for Apartment Blueprint Editor ## Context The editor currently has no concept of construction/design phases. All building elements render unconditionally. This plan adds a phase system so objects can be assigned to phases (e.g., "Demolition", "New Construction", "Finishes") and filtered by three viewing modes. Phases are project-level, user-assignable, and view-only filters — they never alter actual model data. --- ## 1. Domain Model — New Files ### `src/domain/phaseModels.js` (NEW) ```js createPhase(name, order, color) → { id, name, order, color, visible: true } createDefaultPhase() → createPhase('Base Design', 0, '#4A90D9') sortPhases(phases) → sorted by order ascending getOrderedPhases(project) → sortPhases(project.phases || []) getNextPhaseOrder(phases) → max(order) + 1 reorderPhases(phases, movedId, newOrder) → re-indexes all order values PHASE_COLORS = ['#4A90D9', '#E07B39', '#5BAE5B', '#C75D8E', '#8B6EC1', '#D4A843', '#44B4B4', '#D05555'] ``` Uses `generateId('phase')` from `src/domain/ids.js`. ### `src/domain/phaseFilter.js` (NEW) Pure functions — no React dependencies. Single source of truth for filtering. ```js PHASE_VIEW = { ALL: 'all', SINGLE: 'single', CUMULATIVE: 'cumulative' } filterFloorByPhase(floor, phases, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → returns shallow-copy floor with filtered arrays → ALL mode: returns floor unchanged → SINGLE mode: keeps objects where phaseId === activePhaseId or phaseId is null → CUMULATIVE mode: keeps objects where phase.order <= activePhase.order or phaseId is null → Also filters doors/windows whose parent wall was filtered out isObjectVisibleInPhase(obj, phases, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → returns boolean for a single object filterProjectByPhase(project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → returns project with all floors filtered (for 3D/elevation/section) ``` Objects with `phaseId === null` (unassigned) are **always visible** in all modes. ### `src/hooks/usePhaseFilteredFloor.js` (NEW) ```js usePhaseFilteredFloor(floor, project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode) → useMemo wrapping filterFloorByPhase ``` --- ## 2. Model Changes — Existing Files ### `src/domain/models.js` - `createProject`: add `phases: [createDefaultPhase()]` to return object - Import `createDefaultPhase` from `phaseModels.js` - **No changes to individual object factories** — `phaseId` is set at dispatch time ### `src/persistence/deserialize.js` - After existing backfill logic, add: - If `project.phases` is missing/empty, set to `[createDefaultPhase()]` - Validate each phase has `id, name, order, color, visible` - No object-level migration needed — missing `phaseId` treated as `null` (always visible) --- ## 3. State Management ### `src/app/ProjectProvider.jsx` New reducer actions: | Action | Behavior | |--------|----------| | `PHASE_ADD` | Push new phase to `project.phases`, re-sort | | `PHASE_UPDATE` | Merge updates by phase id | | `PHASE_DELETE` | Remove phase; set `phaseId = null` on all objects across all floors that reference it | | `PHASE_REORDER` | Update order values, re-sort | The `PHASE_DELETE` cascade iterates all floors and all object arrays (walls, doors, windows, columns, beams, slabs, stairs, landings, fixtures, rooms, railings) to nullify orphaned `phaseId` references. Pattern follows `FLOOR_DELETE` cascade. ### `src/app/EditorProvider.jsx` New state fields in `createInitialEditorState`: ```js activePhaseId: null, // selected phase for viewing/assigning phaseViewMode: 'all', // 'all' | 'single' | 'cumulative' ``` New reducer actions: - `SET_ACTIVE_PHASE` → sets `activePhaseId` - `SET_PHASE_VIEW_MODE` → sets `phaseViewMode` Add `useEffect` to sync `activePhaseId` when project phases change (similar to `activeFloorId` sync with `availableFloorIds`). Pass `availablePhaseIds` as a prop. --- ## 4. Object Phase Assignment ### `src/editor/useEditorTool.js` - Add `activePhaseId` to the `ctx` object passed to all handlers (line 54-58) - Pull from editor state (already available via `useEditor()` in the call site — `SvgCanvas.jsx`) ### All editor handlers (11 files) Each handler creates objects and dispatches ADD actions. After creating the object, set `phaseId`: ```js // Example in wallDrawHandler.js line 62-66: const wall = createWall(toolState.start, endPt, undefined, { ... }); wall.phaseId = ctx.activePhaseId || null; // ← add this line dispatch({ type: 'WALL_ADD', floorId: activeFloorId, wall }); ``` Files to modify: - `src/editor/handlers/wallDrawHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/doorPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/windowPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/columnPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/beamPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/slabPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/stairPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/landingPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/fixturePlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/roomPlaceHandler.js` - `src/editor/handlers/railingPlaceHandler.js` Also handle auto-detect rooms in Toolbar.jsx (the "Detect Rooms" button). --- ## 5. Rendering Integration ### Plan View — `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` - Import `usePhaseFilteredFloor` - After `const floor = getFloor(activeFloorId)` (line 71), compute: ```js const filteredFloor = usePhaseFilteredFloor(floor, project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode); ``` - Pass `filteredFloor` to all renderers (lines 348-364) instead of `floor` - Keep `floor` (unfiltered) for `SelectionOverlay` and tool previews so selection/interaction still works on all objects - Pass `activePhaseId` and `phaseViewMode` to `useEditorTool` via editor state ### Elevation View — `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` (line 409-416) - Pass filtered project to `ElevationRenderer`: ```jsx ``` - Where `filteredProject` is computed via `filterProjectByPhase(project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode)` ### Section View — `src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx` (line 403-407) - Same pattern: pass `filteredProject` and `filteredFloor` to `SectionRenderer` ### 3D Preview — `src/three/viewer/ThreePreviewPanel.jsx` - Import `filterProjectByPhase` from `phaseFilter.js` - Access `activePhaseId` and `phaseViewMode` from `useEditor()` - Apply filter before building scene descriptor: ```js const filteredProject = useMemo( () => filterProjectByPhase(project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode), [project, activePhaseId, phaseViewMode] ); ``` - Pass `filteredProject` to `buildPreviewScene` (line 35-38) ### Sheet Sources — `src/sheets/sources.js` - `collectPlanPoints` and viewport source builders receive floor data - Apply `filterFloorByPhase` inside `buildPlanSource`, `buildSectionSource`, `buildElevationSource` - Pass phase state through viewport source resolution (from the sheet rendering context) - Sheets initially use the current editor phase view mode; future enhancement can add per-viewport overrides ### Export — `src/export/sheetExport.js` - **No changes needed** — export captures the already-filtered rendered SVG/canvas --- ## 6. UI Changes ### Sidebar — `src/ui/Sidebar.jsx` Add a **"Phases"** collapsible section after "Sheets" and before the floor-context sections. Follows the same section pattern as Floors/Sheets. **Contents:** - Phase view mode segmented control: `[All] [Single] [Cumulative]` - Phase list with each item showing: - Color dot (circle with phase color) - Phase name - Visibility eye toggle - Up/down reorder arrows - Delete button - Click to select as active phase (highlighted with accent border like floor cards) - "+" button to add new phase (auto-named "Phase N", next color from palette) ### Sidebar CSS — `src/ui/Sidebar.module.css` - `.phaseItem` / `.phaseItemActive` — item card styling (follows `.floorCard` pattern) - `.phaseColorDot` — 12px circle with phase color - `.phaseViewControl` — segmented control (follows existing segment pattern) ### Properties Panel — `src/ui/PropertiesPanel.jsx` **PhaseSelector component** (defined inside PropertiesPanel or extracted to separate file): ```jsx function PhaseSelector({ phaseId, phases, onChange }) { return (

input.prompt:
I need to investigate a bug in the phase system of a modular apartment planner. The user reports:

"If I add floors and select phase 2, then when I hop from phase 1 to phase 2 back and forth, it will not show the plan on designated phase."

This means: when switching `activePhaseId` between phases in the sidebar, objects assigned to a particular phase are not showing/hiding correctly in the plan view.

Key areas to investigate thoroughly:

1. **`src/domain/phaseFilter.js`** - The core filtering logic. Check `filterFloorByPhase` and `isObjectVisibleInPhase` - are they correctly handling the `phaseViewMode` and `activePhaseId` parameters? Especially check what happens when `phaseViewMode` is `'all'` (default) vs `'single'` vs `'cumulative'`.

2. **`src/app/EditorProvider.jsx`** - Check the `SET_ACTIVE_PHASE` reducer action - does clicking a phase in the sidebar actually change `activePhaseId`? Also check: does clicking a phase also change `phaseViewMode` from 'all' to 'single'? If `phaseViewMode` stays as `'all'`, filtering would show everything regardless of active phase.

3. **`src/ui/Sidebar.jsx`** - Check the `selectPhase` function and the phase click handler. When the user clicks a phase, what actions are dispatched? Does it only set activePhaseId or does it also change the view mode?

4. **`src/renderers/SvgCanvas.jsx`** - Check how `filteredFloor` is computed and used. Is it actually being passed to renderers?

5. **`src/hooks/usePhaseFilteredFloor.js`** - Check the useMemo dependencies.

The critical question is: **When the user clicks a phase in the sidebar, does `phaseViewMode` remain `'all'`?** If so, `filterFloorByPhase` returns the floor unchanged regardless of which phase is selected, which would explain why switching phases doesn't show/hide objects.

Read all of these files completely and report what you find.

content.text:
Build succeeds. The fix is applied.

**What was wrong:** Clicking a phase in the sidebar set `activePhaseId` but left `phaseViewMode` as `'all'`. The filter logic skips all filtering when mode is `'all'`, so nothing was ever filtered.

**What changed:** Now when you click a phase while in `'all'` mode, it auto-switches to `'single'` mode so only objects assigned to that phase (plus unassigned objects) are shown. If you've manually chosen `'cumulative'` mode, clicking a different phase respects that choice. You can always click the "All" button to go back to seeing everything.
